processing: Validate patrol registry coverage against the process tree.
Validate module patrol blocks and summary-only output boundaries.
Publish dialogue-readable status cards and status events.
Keep process-tree and Obsidian registration synchronized after confirmed patrol changes.
Check future visible module onboarding evidence without enforcing strict mode.
Enforce strict required patrol coverage for process-tree visible modules.
Run patrol-maintainer equivalent workflow to validate, publish, sync, gate and expose read surfaces.
Compute source_hash values from non-runtime module source refs and detect source drift before current-state claims.
Provide scaffold entry for new process-tree modules to receive patrol registry and block coverage.
Expose a unified enforcement command for CI and local pre-commit workflows.
Require append-only module build timelines for visible modules before module gate current-state claims.
Aggregate latest build timeline phase/status into the dialogue read index.
Expose construction depth so dialogue can distinguish shell, contract, data flow, runtime surface, validation and dialogue surface changes.

state.headline: Strict coverage, source drift detection and construction-time build timeline feedback are active.
state.current_task: Use system-patrol:timeline, source-drift and maintain as the required feedback loop for visible module construction and modification.
risks: Process-tree-derived patrol blocks are summary coverage, not deep source-code interpretations of every module.
Strict mode blocks missing patrol evidence but does not prove business behavior correctness by itself.
Local pre-commit installation depends on a valid git repository; this workspace currently needs git validation before hooks can be installed.
Source drift detection proves source-ref freshness for patrol baselines, not deep business behavior correctness.
Build timeline events prove patrol feedback continuity, not business behavior correctness.
Dialogue timeline visibility depends on runtime/dialogue-system-patrol/dialogue-read-index.json being regenerated after timeline updates.
Local pre-commit installation is blocked when D:/zhineng is not a valid git repository.
next: Run npm.cmd run system-patrol:maintain -- --module-id=<module_id> after visible module changes.
Run npm.cmd run system-patrol:maintain -- --all for global patrol refresh.
Use user confirmation before protocol migration, business-module code migration, dialogue-reader code changes, or non-source-only 3D behavior.
Run npm.cmd run system-patrol:source-drift -- --update --module-id=<module_id> after reviewed module source or patrol edits.
Use npm.cmd run system-patrol:scaffold -- --module-id=<module_id> --verify after adding a new process-tree module.
Use npm.cmd run system-patrol:enforce for CI/pre-commit all-module verification.
Record a build timeline event before module gate claims current state.
Regenerate dialogue read surfaces after timeline, source drift or module gate changes.

forbidden_actions: no business module rewrite in this registration step
no dialogue reader rewrite in this registration step
no strict-mode migration without user confirmation
no business behavior correctness claim from patrol coverage alone
no source_hash baseline refresh before reviewing the relevant module or patrol change
no claim that CI/pre-commit hooks are installed when git repository validation fails
no claim that construction-time feedback is active when build timeline output is missing or stale
confirmation_gates: user confirmation required for protocol migration
user confirmation required before strict required coverage
user confirmation required before adding a true patrol-maintainer subagent
user confirmation required before changing dialogue reader source contracts
user confirmation required before replacing source_hash algorithm semantics

may_say: The patrol layer is registered in the process tree.
The patrol publisher writes summary-only status cards and events.
Current module patrol coverage is validated when validation reports have no required or warning failures.
The module onboarding gate can report whether a visible module has patrol and validation evidence.
Strict required coverage is enabled for registered process-tree modules.
The patrol-maintainer workflow can refresh validation, publication, module gate and read surfaces.
The patrol layer can detect source_hash drift from declared source refs.
The scaffold entry can create patrol coverage for process-tree modules.
The CI/pre-commit entry runs the same all-module patrol enforcement workflow.
The patrol layer can expose each module latest build timeline phase/status through the dialogue read index.
The module gate blocks current-state claims when the build timeline feedback loop is missing, invalid or stale.
must_not_infer: Do not claim every module has deep source-code interpretation from summary patrol blocks alone.
Do not claim business-module health from missing or stale patrol evidence.
Do not treat the patrol registry as a replacement for the process tree.
Do not treat module_onboarding_ready as proof of deep business behavior correctness.
Do not claim strict patrol mode proves product behavior correctness beyond patrol evidence.
Do not claim 3D projection has write or execution authority.
Do not claim source_hash freshness if source drift reports are missing or blocked.
Do not claim local pre-commit enforcement is installed unless hook installation succeeds.
Do not claim construction progress if module_build_timeline_event.v1 is missing or stale.
Do not treat a build timeline event as proof of business behavior correctness without validation refs.
